pi_items.source: task.json description and all task scripts (setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, verifier.py) consistently reference the role name 'data_analyst'

OrientDB documentation confirms 'reader' is a built-in security role. See: https://orientdb.gitbooks.io/orientdb-manual/content/orientdb.wiki/Security.html and task.json step 1 explicitly states inheritance from 'reader' role
task.json description step 4 and export_result.sh line 67 and 99 consistently reference user 'maria_garcia'
export_result.sh line 99 uses this exact password in the authentication test curl command: -u 'maria_garcia:Analyst2024!'

OrientDB documentation confirms permission code 1 = CREATE. See: https://orientdb.gitbooks.io/orientdb-manual/content/orientdb.wiki/Security.html (permission bitmasks: CREATE = #0001 = 1). export_result.sh line 56 checks 'has_create(rules.get('database.class', 0))' for this permission
Same as above - CREATE permission code is 1. export_result.sh line 57 checks 'has_create(rules.get('database.cluster', 0))' for this permission

OrientDB documentation confirms 'database.class' is a valid resource format for permissions. See: https://orientdb.com/docs/last/sql/SQL-Grant.html and https://orientdb.dev/docs/3.1.x/security/Database-Security.html
OrientDB documentation confirms 'database.cluster' is a valid resource format for permissions. See: https://orientdb.com/docs/last/sql/SQL-Grant.html
task.json step 4 specifies status 'ACTIVE'. OrientDB documentation confirms ACTIVE and SUSPENDED are valid user statuses. See: https://orientdb.dev/docs/3.0.x/security/Database-Security.html

privileged_info_summary: The task creates a custom OrientDB security role named 'data_analyst' that inherits from the built-in 'reader' role with permission mode 0 (deny-all-but). It grants CREATE (permission code 1) access on resources 'database.class' and 'database.cluster'. A database user 'maria_garcia' is created with password 'Analyst2024!' and status 'ACTIVE', assigned to the 'data_analyst' role. All metadata claims have been verified against OrientDB documentation and the task implementation scripts.
